title: Two-Tier Plywood Jewelry Box
summary: A small hinged jewelry box built entirely from 1/2" plywood, with a shallow lid tray sitting on a deeper base.
description:
This box is two separate open boxes joined by a hinge — a shallow lid tray on top of a deeper base box — rather than one box with a lid cut off of it, which keeps the grain and edge banding continuous around each piece instead of showing a saw kerf where a lid was parted off.

Every piece is 1/2" plywood, joined with a **micro pocket-hole jig** sized for stock this thin — a standard-size jig is made for 3/4" material and will blow through the face of a piece this size. Plywood edges are covered with ironed-on edge banding rather than left raw, which is what keeps a small, close-up piece like this from reading as "a box made of plywood."

tools.note: Or a miter saw — breaks down the plywood.
A micro jig sized for 1/2" stock.

materials.name: 1/2" plywood, partial sheet
materials.note: One sheet yields several boxes; buy a full sheet even for one.

steps.title: Cut all the pieces
steps.body: Cut every plywood piece for both the top (lid) box and the bottom box per the cut list.

steps.title: Drill the pocket holes
steps.body: Drill two pocket holes along each side of the top and bottom panels using a micro jig sized for 1/2" stock — a standard jig is set up for thicker material and will blow through a panel this thin.

steps.title: Assemble both boxes
steps.body: Attach the four side pieces to the bottom panel with pocket screws to build the bottom box, reinforcing with brad nails if needed. Build the top (lid) box the same way.

steps.title: Band the edges
steps.body: Iron edge banding onto every exposed plywood edge, trim the overhang with a utility knife, and sand it flush.

steps.title: Fill, sand, and finish
steps.body: Fill any tear-out or exposed pocket holes with wood filler, sand smooth, then paint or stain both boxes.

steps.title: Hinge and finish out
steps.body: Attach the hinges and latch to join the lid box to the base. Optionally glue in a small mirror and a ribbon pull-tab.
